## 2026-05-24 вҖ” Fix InteractiveCanvas tap/drag regression (BaseCanvas wrapped dragging setters)
**Commit:** `6f7158f7` вҖ” merge of `fix/basecanvas-dragging-ref` (`009de46c`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed вҖ” surgical 1-file fix; restores tap-element + drag-element on all 4 InteractiveCanvas consumers (MatchPage scouting, TacticPage, BunkerEditorPage, LayoutDetailPage).

**What changed:** Fix InteractiveCanvas tap/drag regression вҖ” restore wrapped dragging setters in BaseCanvas (ref froze at null since Step #2 extraction `ecc850ce`, live since Step #4). Affected tap-element + drag on MatchPage / Tactic / BunkerEditor / LayoutDetail.

**Root cause:** `BaseCanvas.jsx:172-177` used raw `useState` setters for `dragging` / `draggingBunker` while keeping `draggingRef` / `draggingBunkerRef` side-by-side. `touchHandler.js` reads the **refs** (handleMove:338,444 for drag-player; handleUp:471,614 for tap-detection), so `setDragging(hit)` in handleDown updated React state but the ref stayed frozen at `null`. Tap-element (toolbar open) and drag-element (move) both died silently; zoom/pan/place stayed alive because they don't depend on draggingRef. The pan path even won what should have been drag-player gestures (`if panStartRef && dragging === null && draggingBunker === null`) вҶ’ "drag dead, pan wins" symptom matched exactly. Same bug applied to bunker drag.

**Bug provenance:** introduced structurally at Step #2 BaseCanvas extraction (`ecc850ce`, 2026-05-23) but dormant вҖ” no consumer rendered BaseCanvas. Went live at Step #4 InteractiveCanvas migration (`71179616`, 2026-05-24) when 4 consumers moved onto BaseCanvas. Step #2's "additive only, bundle hash bit-identical" claim was true at deploy time but the latent bug shipped under it.

**Fix:** Restore wrapped-setter pattern from `FieldCanvas:81-86` **1:1**:
```js
const [dragging, _setDragging] = useState(null);
const [draggingBunker, _setDraggingBunker] = useState(null);
const draggingRef = useRef(null);
const draggingBunkerRef = useRef(null);
const setDragging = (v) => { draggingRef.current = v; _setDragging(v); };
const setDraggingBunker = (v) => { draggingBunkerRef.current = v; _setDraggingBunker(v); };
```
State preserved (eslint-disable matches legacy). **No state-drop** in this PR per Opus instruction вҖ” keeps draw-effect dep surface unchanged on the hot path; state-drop cleanup deferable separately if ever worth doing.

**Off-limits untouched** (`git diff --name-only` = `BaseCanvas.jsx`, nothing else): `touchHandler.js`, `InteractiveCanvas.jsx`, the 4 consumers (MatchPage / TacticPage / BunkerEditorPage / LayoutDetailPage), `FieldCanvas.jsx` (legacy for BallisticsPage), `ballisticsEngine.js`. No schema / dataService / rules change.

**В§ 75 unblock:** Per В§ 75 sequencing "regres fix NAJPIERW", this clears the runway for full-screen (#11 generalized) + DrawingOverlay impl. Gesture grammar in BaseCanvas now consistent (ref-state sync); per-screen drift root removed structurally.

**Anti-pattern codified:** `PROJECT_GUIDELINES.md В§ 9 Architektura` вҖ” new bullet: "gesture state read by touchHandler via ref MUSI have wrapped setter updating ref + state; raw useState setter freezes ref вҶ’ silent tap/drag death." Cites the bug + fix SHA for future archaeology.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ 7.48s clean; `npm run precommit` вң“ all checks passed (baseline warnings only вҖ” amber/chevron/TODO refs in unrelated files). Main bundle `index.js` 227.89 kB / gzip 68.55 kB вҖ” unchanged (literal-equivalent edit, no minified delta).

**Post-deploy smoke (Jacek, prod):**
1. MatchPage scouting вҶ’ tap an existing player marker вҶ’ inline toolbar (Assign/Hit/Shot/Del) opens.
2. MatchPage scouting вҶ’ drag an existing player вҶ’ marker follows finger; canvas does **NOT** pan.
3. MatchPage scouting вҶ’ tap empty space вҶ’ places new player (regression check).
4. MatchPage scouting вҶ’ pinch-zoom + pan with no selection вҶ’ still work (regression check).
5. BunkerEditorPage вҶ’ drag a bunker anchor вҶ’ bunker follows finger; canvas does **NOT** pan.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 6f7158f7 && git push && npm run deploy`. Single-shot revert. (Note: rollback re-introduces the regression вҖ” only roll back if the fix itself causes a new symptom.)

## 2026-05-24 вҖ” Training guest squad-persist fix (invite-time + auto-distribute)
**Commit:** `909e7105` вҖ” merge of `fix/training-guest-squad-persist` (`6b9bd55b`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed вҖ” narrow data-layer fix; no UI surfaces touched.

**What changed:** Fixes the "Bez skЕӮadu" / "unassigned" bucket in `TrainingCoachTab` for invited guests. Two complementary write-path changes so every attendee ends up in some squad in Firestore, regardless of invite order.

**Root cause (per discovery):** Invite-guest path wrote to `training.attendees[]` only вҖ” `squads{}` was untouched. `SquadEditor`'s mount-time auto-distribute existed (lines 27-49) and computed a corrected `next` squads locally, but never persisted via `scheduleSave` вҖ” opening the editor and navigating away without dragging anyone threw the recovery away. Coach summary groups attendees by `training.squads[*]` membership вҶ’ guests with `squadKey === null` landed in the `'other'` bucket labelled "Bez skЕӮadu" (`TrainingCoachTab.jsx:184-202`).

**Option 1 вҖ” `AttendeesEditor.jsx` (atomic invite-time placement, +49/вҲ’9 LOC):**
- New `placeIntoExistingSquads(baseSquads, pidsToPlace)` helper вҖ” picks smallest existing squad with `SQUAD_META` tie-break (red вҶ’ blue вҶ’ green вҶ’ yellow вҶ’ purple order). Idempotent (skips pids already in any squad). Returns null when no squads exist yet вҖ” Option 2 catches that path.
- `toggleAttendee` ADD branch: combines `{ attendees, squads }` into single `updateTraining` write вҖ” guest lands in smallest squad immediately, no reliance on SquadEditor being opened. REMOVE branch unchanged (still uses existing `syncSquads`).
- `applyPreset`: prunes squads of removed pids first (existing `syncSquads`), then places newly-added pids on top of the cleaned squads. Idempotent for pids already placed.
- Imports `SQUADS as SQUAD_META` from `utils/squads`.

**Option 2 вҖ” `SquadEditor.jsx` (persist auto-distribute on mount, +37/вҲ’10 LOC):**
- New file-level `squadsDiffer(a, b)` helper (set-equal per key) вҖ” gates the persist so re-renders don't schedule no-op writes.
- Moved `scheduleSave` + cleanup ABOVE the mount effect so the effect can reference `scheduleSave` in its deps.
- Mount effect (line ~27): after the existing round-robin auto-distribute (logic byte-for-byte unchanged), if `squadsDiffer(initial, next)` вҶ’ `scheduleSave(next)`. Effect deps gain `scheduleSave` (stable per `trainingId`, already in deps via `training?.id`).

**How both compose:**
| Invite order | Persistence path |
|---|---|
| Squads already exist вҶ’ invite guest | Option 1 вҖ” atomic single-write |
| Invite attendees first вҶ’ form squads | Option 2 вҖ” first SquadEditor mount detects diff, schedules save |
| Belt-and-braces (invite #1, form, invite #2, open) | Option 1 places #2; Option 2 sees no diff, skips |

**Off-limits вҖ” verified untouched** (`git diff --name-only` = AttendeesEditor.jsx + SquadEditor.jsx, nothing more):
- `TrainingCoachTab.jsx` вҖ” `'other'` bucket label + grouping logic intact; we stop putting people in it
- `TrainingScoutTab.jsx` вҖ” matchup roster snapshot (`:99-101` / `:111-112`) untouched
- `PlayerStatsPage` / `usePPTIdentity` / `usePlayers` / `playersById` identity layer
- Canvas (`BaseCanvas`/`InteractiveCanvas`/`HeatmapCanvas`/`FieldCanvas`), `BallisticsPage`, `ballisticsEngine.js`
- Schema, `dataService`, Firestore rules, new collections

**В§ 27:** PASS вҖ” write-path only, no UI surfaces touched. Auto-placement was the existing В§ 32 / В§ 53 UX-intended state; we persist it now instead of throwing it away.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ 5.06s; `npm run precommit` вң“ all checks passed (baseline warnings only вҖ” amber/chevron/TODO refs in unrelated files). Main bundle `index.js` 227.89 kB / gzip 68.54 kB вҖ” unchanged vs Step #5 baseline.

**Smoke (Jacek):**
1. Trening z istniejД…cymi skЕӮadami вҶ’ zaproЕӣ goЕӣcia z AttendeesEditor вҶ’ otwГіrz Coach summary вҶ’ goЕӣДҮ pod jakimЕӣ skЕӮadem (nie "Bez skЕӮadu").
2. Nowy trening вҶ’ zaproЕӣ kilku attendees PRZED uformowaniem skЕӮadГіw вҶ’ otwГіrz SquadEditor вҶ’ po ~300 ms (debounce) wszyscy w skЕӮadach po reloadzie.
3. Free-play matchup regresja: stats goЕӣcia nadal liczone (free-play roster = attendees, niezaleЕјne od squads).
4. Sentry: zero nowych errorГіw na invite / SquadEditor mount.

**Known limitation (out of scope вҖ” separate ticket if Jacek wants):**
- Squad-vs-squad matchupy utworzone PRZED zaproszeniem goЕӣcia majД… zamroЕјony `homeRoster`/`awayRoster` snapshot (`TrainingScoutTab.jsx:99-101 / :111-112`) вҶ’ goЕӣДҮ NIE pojawi siДҷ w nich wstecznie (zero punktГіw w tych konkretnych matchupach). Fix dotyczy: przyszЕӮych squad-matchupГіw + free-play + etykiety "Bez skЕӮadu" w coach summary. Backfill starych squad-matchup rosterГіw = osobny temat.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 909e7105 && git push && npm run deploy`. Single-shot revert of both option's write-path changes.

## 2026-05-24 вҖ” В§ 64.9 Step #5: HeatmapCanvas вҶ’ BaseCanvas + FieldView deprecation
**Commit:** `cb28a26a` вҖ” merge of `feat/canvas-step5-heatmapcanvas` (`5d640716`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed вҖ” read-only consumer migration + dispatcher delete. **NOT hot-path** (HeatmapCanvas had zero gestures before; migration adds pinch+pan default-off вҖ” pure addition, removes nothing).

**What changed:** В§ 64.9 step #5 вҖ” HeatmapCanvas refactored **in-place** onto BaseCanvas. DOM/DPR/sizing/RO/landscape now owned by BaseCanvas; ~300 LOC draw body moved verbatim to BaseCanvas's `draw` render-prop callback (plain arrow function вҖ” new ref each render вҶ’ BaseCanvas's draw effect re-fires via deps array вҶ’ closure refresh вҶ’ toggle props repaint canvas). Matches Step #4 InteractiveCanvas pattern. **В§ 64.9 step #8 (FieldView deprecation) collapsed into step #5** in same PR вҖ” after Step #4, FieldView's non-heatmap branch was dead code; pure-passthrough audit confirmed all 3 call sites = variant (a) straight delete (no Btn UI port, no style/className forwarding lost).

**Gestures (В§ 64.4):** `pinchZoom` + `pan` opt-in via prop, default off (matches today's no-gesture behavior; landscape coach view step #11 will flip them on for ScoutedTeamPage). **Loupe NEVER** вҖ” naturally inert via two existing consumer-side gates: (a) `touchHandler.js:178,352` `setActiveTouchPos` requires `editable||layoutEditMode` which HeatmapCanvas never passes via `touchHandlerState`; (b) `drawLoupe` called only from `FieldCanvas:335` + `InteractiveCanvas:236`, not from HeatmapCanvas's draw layer. Zero touchHandler changes вҖ” discovery STEP 2 (in chat) proved loupe-off is free; option (b) chosen over a defensive guard or split.

**Sizing (corrected from brief STEP 3 wording during impl):** **width-first via BaseCanvas, no `maxCanvasHeight` cap**. Brief originally specified `sizingStrategy='height-first' maxCanvasHeight=canvasMaxHeight(200,200)` but tracing BaseCanvas:144-158 math showed this would render 1000Г—500 canvases in portrait вҶ’ overflow:hidden clip вҶ’ left-cropped half-field zoom (broken read-equivalence). Today's `HeatmapCanvas:34-39` `min(aspectH, maxH)` reduces to width-first in portrait (`aspectH=175 вүӘ maxH=500`). Width-first now matches today's portrait verbatim. Landscape letterbox cap (today's `min(aspectH, maxH)` when `aspectH > maxH`) is dead code in step #5 scope (landscape activation = step #11); may need width-first-with-cap added to BaseCanvas API at step #11. Deviation documented in HeatmapCanvas header docblock.

**3 FieldView call sites migrated to direct `<HeatmapCanvas>`:**
- `ScoutedTeamPage:654` (collapsed preview, 110px clip outer)
- `ScoutedTeamPage:674` (expanded view)
- `TrainingResultsPage:376` (source-filtered training heatmap with All/Scout/Coach/Player pills)

**MatchPage:1413 (direct caller, pre-existing)** code untouched вҖ” props 1:1 compatible with new HeatmapCanvas signature (new `pinchZoom`/`pan` defaults preserve no-gesture rendering). Renderer changed вҶ’ **#1 priority post-deploy smoke** (scouting hot-path; heatmap tab in live match).

**FieldView.jsx DELETED** (207 LOC). After Step #4 its non-heatmap branch had zero callers; only used through `mode='heatmap'` dispatch which now goes direct.

**DPR `Г—2` hardcoded literal at `HeatmapCanvas:49`** REMOVED вҖ” BaseCanvas owns runtime `window.devicePixelRatio || 2` per В§ 64.8.5. One of the 3 sites flagged in В§ 64.11 finally migrates with its owning consumer.

**Off-limits invariants verified untouched:** `FieldCanvas.jsx`, `InteractiveCanvas.jsx`, `BaseCanvas.jsx`, `BallisticsPage.jsx`, `ballisticsEngine.js`, `touchHandler.js`, `drawLoupe.js`, `./field/draw*`, `MatchPage.jsx`.

**В§ 27:** PASS вҖ” zero new UI surfaces; all existing color/typography/elevation/touch decisions preserved verbatim (gradients = data viz not decoration; HERO amber ring preserved; kill рҹ’Җ + danger zone preserved). Frame styling moved canvas-element вҶ’ BaseCanvas inner-frame div (2px borderRadius delta `RADIUS.lg=12 вҶ’ 10` same as Step #4 which shipped).

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (5.59s); `npm run precommit` вң“ all checks passed (baseline warnings only вҖ” pre-existing amber/chevron nudges + 5 TODO refs in unrelated files). 4 files changed, +85/вҲ’275 LOC net `вҲ’190 LOC`. Bundle: `index.js` 227.89 kB / gzip 68.55 kB (was 228.50 / 68.63 вҖ” **вҲ’0.61 kB / вҲ’0.08 kB gzip** net; FieldView delete savings barely offset HeatmapCanvas slight growth + MatchPage chunk +0.08 kB from refactored module import).

## 2026-05-23 вҖ” Rules tighten: selfReports cross-pid (В§ 49.10, audit gap #2)
**Commit:** `c2fb9ba` вҖ” merge of `fix/rules-selfreports-cross-pid-tighten` (`3d78b8a`)
**Rules deployed:** `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` ran first (live `pbscoutpro` rules updated, "released rules firestore.rules to cloud.firestore"), then merge + `npm run deploy`. **Two-step deploy** (rules + bundle); next time same pattern.
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** closes audit gap #2 вҖ” `/workspaces/{slug}/players/{pid}/selfReports/{sid}` was gated on `isPlayer(slug)` only; any workspace player could write any pid's logs (theoretical, contained by invited-only workspace). Now:
- **CREATE** = `isLinkedSelfPlayer(slug, pid)` вҖ” the writer must be the parent player's `linkedUid`. No coach carve-out (KIOSK writes `point.selfLogs[]` via `setPlayerSelfLogTraining`, not `/selfReports/`; propagator stamps `_meta` on the POINT вҖ” never creates a selfReport).
- **UPDATE / DELETE** = `isCoach(slug) OR isLinkedSelfPlayer(slug, pid)` вҖ” coach carve-out is **required**: В§ 70.2 matcher write-back (`propagateMatchup` writes `{slotRef, propagatedAt}` + low-conf `{needsReview, candidateSlotRef}`), В§ 70.11 Stage 4 `applySelfReportOverride` + `dismissSelfReportFlag` all run in the coach's session and legitimately update other players' selfReports. A bare check would have BROKEN every matcher run and every Stage 4 action.
- New helper **`isLinkedSelfPlayer(slug, pid)`** with `exists()`-guard + brittle-null-safe `data.get('linkedUid', null)` (matches the convention from the player self-link rule at L239).

**Audit trail:** PRE-FLIGHT enumerated every selfReport write path before applying the rule (see В§ 49.10 in DESIGN_DECISIONS). Out-of-scope notes (separate brief): the `isSelfLogShotCreate` `playerId` field-claim (rules header L12-15) вҖ” affects shots, not selfReports.

**В§ 27:** N/A вҖ” rules-only change.

**Validation:** rules compile вң“ (Firebase CLI confirmed "rules file firestore.rules compiled successfully" pre-release); `vite build` вң“ (7.91s); `lint-ui` 0 errors. No JS changes вҖ” same bundle.

**Smoke (post-deploy):**
- Linked player PPT-logs to own pid вҶ’ вң… create allowed.
- Attempt to write `/players/{otherPid}/selfReports/` as a non-coach (via DevTools / SDK) вҶ’ вқҢ permission-denied.
- Close a training вҶ’ `propagateMatchup` updates selfReports cleanly (no rules-deny in Sentry).
- TrainingResultsPage вҶ’ "Needs review" вҶ’ Accept / Dismiss вҶ’ updates land.

**Rollback:** rules вҖ” `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` against the pre-merge `firestore.rules` (checkout the old version first); code вҖ” `git revert -m 1 c2fb9ba && git push && npm run deploy`. Rules revert is the load-bearing step; code is docs-only.

## 2026-05-23 вҖ” Fix: touchHandler close-toolbar ReferenceError (Sentry-reported)
**Commit:** `e4f188f` вҖ” merge of `fix/touchhandler-on-toolbar-action-ref` (`4edef48`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** `src/components/field/touchHandler.js:309` (`handleDown`) referenced **bare** `onToolbarAction?.(...)` вҖ” undeclared in `handleDown`'s closure scope (the destructure at L462 is local to `handleUp`; the top-level destructure L17вҖ“26 doesn't include it). Optional chaining (`?.()`) does **not** protect against undeclared identifiers вҖ” only against `null`/`undefined` values вҖ” so every empty-canvas-tap with a player toolbar open threw `ReferenceError: onToolbarAction is not defined`, that close-path failed silently (user could still close via the React backdrop overlay or a toolbar button), and Sentry alarmed.

**Fix (one line):** `onToolbarAction?.(...)` вҶ’ `stateRef.current.onToolbarAction?.(...)` вҖ” matches the existing convention at the same file's L555 and `stateRef.current.onEmptyTap` at L311. + a short comment explaining the trap so the regression doesn't repeat.

**Diagnosis correction.** `NEXT_TASKS:39` had hypothesised "undefined prop under a mount sequence" вҖ” that was wrong; the prop was always defined at the call site (`MatchPage:1852`, `TacticPage:460` both pass `handleToolbarAction`). The error was an **undeclared identifier in `handleDown`'s closure**, completely unrelated to mount sequence. NEXT_TASKS:39 updated.

**Impact:** kills the Sentry alarm + restores the empty-canvas-tap-while-toolbar-open close path. Hot-path-adjacent (FieldCanvas is the scouting hot path вҖ” MatchPage / TacticPage / heatmap).

**В§ 27:** N/A вҖ” pure logic in a non-React module; no visual change.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (7.71s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`.

**Smoke:** open a player toolbar on a match/tactic вҶ’ tap empty canvas (not the backdrop, not a button) вҶ’ toolbar closes cleanly, no console / Sentry error. Backdrop + button close paths unchanged.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 e4f188f && git push && npm run deploy`.

**Commit:** `bf65242` вҖ” merge of `fix/team-delete-to-retire` (`29da63e`, `2a26e65`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** closes the Phase 2.3.d global/workspace mismatch confirmed by `b9f9bc1`.
- **UI "delete team" вҶ’ `retireTeam`** at both callers: `TeamDetailPage:117` (`handleDeleteTeam`) and `TeamsPage:66` (`handleDelete`). Old `deleteTeam` was workspace-only while `useTeams` reads global вҶ’ orphans. Retire is soft (sets `retiredAt`), dual-writes global+workspace, recoverable by an admin; `useActiveTeams` filters retired so the team disappears from every user-facing list (audit: 23 consumers via `useActiveTeams`; only `AdminTeamsPage` reads raw `useTeams`).
- **`deleteTeam` retained** in `dataService` as the super_admin-only hard-delete (firestore.rules `/teams/{id}` `delete: if isSuperAdmin()`) вҖ” AdminTeamsPage path.
- **ConfirmModal copy fixed** (both modals). Was: *"DeleteвҖҰ Players will not be deleted but will become unassigned."* вҖ” misleading even under the old code (`deleteTeam` never touched player docs). Now: *"X will be removed from your teams. Scouted data is preserved and an admin can restore the team."*
- **Orphan cleanup (one-shot, post-deploy).** Hard-deleted the 1 confirmed orphan `7rXJ0Z0U3h4wBAaoZzo8` ("test team 123123- SKASUJ MNIEEEEEE", originWorkspace ranger1996). Pre-clean: global=299, ws=298, orphans=1. Post-clean: global=298, ws=298, diff=0. Sweep ran twice (matches diagnosis count + workspace counterpart confirmed absent) before delete; aborts if state drifted.

**В§ 27:** PASS вҖ” `ConfirmModal` reused; `danger` flag preserved; honest copy (no false "permanent"); `Delete` label valid (read sites filter retired).

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (7.47s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`. DB diff verified pre+post.

**Smoke (to do on device):** delete a team via TeamDetailPage / TeamsPage вҶ’ it disappears from every active list; AdminTeamsPage retired view shows it; unretire restores it.

**Out of scope (deferred):** PL i18n on the new copy (current modal is plain English вҖ” matches existing pattern); children-orphan warning on the simple delete (AdminTeamsPage has it; simple modal retires a parent without cascading вҖ” children stay parented).

## 2026-05-22 вҖ” В§ 70 Stage 4: manual override review queue вҖ” Track C COMPLETE (В§ 70.11)
**Commit:** `e5d963e` вҖ” merge of `feat/stage4-manual-override` (`5f72ec3`, `10bfbcf`, `5b81c34`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** the last element of Track C вҖ” the human review surface for low-confidence matcher results.
- **"Needs review"** section on `TrainingResultsPage` (between the leaderboard and "Break bunkers"), **coach/admin-gated**, shown only when the flagged queue is non-empty. Per item: player + observation (reuses `LogRow`) + the matcher-proposed point вҖ” actions **Accept #N** / **Reassign to #N** / **Dismiss**.
- `applySelfReportOverride` вҖ” Accept/Reassign reuse `propagateSelfReportToPoint` + stamp `{slotRef, propagatedAt, needsReview:false}`; the selfReport observation is never rewritten.
- `dismissSelfReportFlag` вҖ” sets a **sticky `reviewDismissedAt`**; `propagateMatchup` now skips on `propagatedAt` **OR** `reviewDismissedAt`, so a training re-close never re-flags a dismissed report (both kept in `alignSequence` input вҶ’ pairing stays stable).
- `getTrainingSelfReports` вҖ” collectionGroup fetch; the review queue resolves candidate point/slot + reassign options by re-running the pure matcher (`locatePlayerInPoint`/`alignSequence`) in preview mode.
- Out of scope v1 (documented В§ 70.11): re-litigating already-propagated matches (no inverse-propagate); orphan-promotion (needs a lineup edit).

**рҹҺү В§ 70 / Track C / Klocek 2 вҖ” COMPLETE.** Stages 1, 1b, 2, 3 (D1+D2), 4 all shipped + deployed.

**В§ 27:** PASS вҖ” `SectionLabel` + `LogRow` reused; Accept=accent / DismissВ·Reassign=default; theme tokens; no competing CTA.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (7.71s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`.

**Smoke:** training with a low-confidence flagged self-log вҶ’ "Needs review" lists it вҶ’ Accept writes it into the point (leaves queue, shows on heatmap/leaderboard) вҶ’ Reassign lands on the chosen point вҶ’ Dismiss leaves the queue + survives a training re-close (not re-flagged).

**Commit:** `5cf783a` вҖ” merge of `fix/samoocena-empty-state-gate` (`a119a0e`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** follow-up fix to В§ 70.9. The "Samoocena" section was placed *inside* `PlayerStatsPage`'s `stats.played > 0` block вҖ” so a player with self-logs but **zero scouted coach points** (the common case вҖ” they self-logged more than the coach lineup'd them) hit the "No scouted points yet" empty state and **never saw the section**. That is exactly the scenario В§ 70.9 was built for.
- Fix: "Samoocena" is now a **sibling** of the `stats.played > 0` block, gated only on `selfReports.length > 0` вҖ” renders independently of coach-side stats.
- The "No scouted points yet" empty state now also requires `selfReports.length === 0`, so it no longer covers a player who has self-logs.
- Diagnosed from prod data: the reporting user is player **Koe** (`linkedUid` = their account); their 4 self-logs for the closed training "tesyt" (+ 47 total) were correctly stored вҖ” the section was simply unreachable.

**В§ 27:** N/A вҖ” section placement only.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (8.58s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`.

## 2026-05-22 вҖ” D1 heatmap: player self-log dot placement fix (В§ 70.10)
**Commit:** `b500973` вҖ” merge of `fix/d1-self-log-placement` (`6653153`, `8ebcd56`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** player self-log dots on the D1 training heatmap ("Player" pill) were landing at the **mirror-image bunker**. Root cause: a player self-log dot is **bunker-derived** вҖ” the propagator stores `bunkerToPosition(bunker)` = `bunker.x Вұ 0.02, bunker.y`, a bunker-**absolute** coord вҖ” but D1's builder ran it through `mirrorPointToLeft` (xвҶ’1-x) along with the real, team-relative scout/coach coords, flipping it to the opposite bunker.
- **Fix (path a, render-scoped):** for slots `playersMeta[i].source вҲҲ {self,kiosk}`, `resolveSelfLogDot()` takes the **un-mirrored** synth, reverse-looks-up the nearest layout bunker (`field.bunkers`), and re-places at `bunkerToPosition(bunker,'left')` вҖ” conventional LEFT (player gave no start side), un-mirrored. Scout/coach slots unchanged.
- **Tie-guard:** real layouts' tightest bunker spacing is 0.0506 (NXL Tampa) вҖ” safe; but the "2026 sample layout" has a 0.0028 near-duplicate, so the guard snaps only when the nearest bunker is вүӨ0.04 away AND beats the runner-up by вүҘ0.012, else keeps the un-mirrored synth (benign вҖ” still the right bunker).
- Direction-only logs (unresolved bunker) вҶ’ no stored coord вҶ’ not rendered (exclusion free). **Stored data untouched** вҖ” propagator coord stays correct for `positionConfidence`.
- **Deferred вҖ” path (b):** reading `selfReports` directly would also surface orphan self-logs on the heatmap (Samoocena-consistent) вҖ” a coverage/product decision, separate from this placement fix.

**В§ 27:** N/A вҖ” pure coord-math in a `useMemo`; no visual-system surface touched.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (9.73s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`.

**Smoke:** "test training (PROD)" вҶ’ Heatmap вҶ’ Player pill вҶ’ Koe's dots beside their logged bunkers on the LEFT (not the mirror-image spot); Scout/Coach dots unchanged; unresolved-bunker log вҶ’ no dot.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 b500973 && git push && npm run deploy`.

---

## 2026-05-22 вҖ” "Samoocena": player self-logs on the profile (В§ 70.9)
**Commit:** `194c755` вҖ” merge of `feat/samoocena-self-report-section` (`4bfd470`, `9e10a8a`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** new **"Samoocena"** section on `PlayerStatsPage` (after "Historia meczГіw") вҖ” lists the player's own `selfReports` (`players/{pid}/selfReports`), **ALL of them вҖ” matched + orphan**, separate from coach-observed W/L.
- Solves the gap diagnosed earlier: a player's self-logs that don't reconcile to a coach point (orphan) were invisible everywhere. Per Jacek's call, orphan self-logs = the player's **self-assessment** вҖ” now surfaced on the profile.
- `getSelfReportsForPlayer(playerId, trainingId)` (`playerPerformanceTrackerService.js`) вҖ” per-player subcollection read, fetch-all + client-filter. **No collectionGroup, no composite index.**
- Row UI **reuses `LogRow`** (exported from `components/ppt/TodaysLogsList.jsx`) вҖ” no duplicate row UI.
- Visible in `scope=training` (filtered by `tid`) + `scope=global` (all, flat chronological); hidden in `tournament`/`match` scope and when the player has no self-logs.

All ~9 roster-read sites converted `p.teamId===X` вҶ’ `playerOnTeam()`.
- **Import вҖ” pbliId = authoritative cross-team key.** `CSVImport`: a row whose `pbliId` matches an existing player **appends** the import team to `teams[]` (dedupe; never overwrites `teamId`/name/profile). **Name-match never cross-appends** (Chavez US вү  Chavez EU); no-pbliId rows keep the existing within-team name-dedup **unchanged** (no regression). `addPlayer` persists `teams[]`.
- **`PlayerEditModal`** вҖ” teams[] editor (chip rows: вҳ… set-primary / name / вң• remove + "add team" picker) вҖ” the manual multi-membership path.
- Parent/child: `teams[]` is direct-only; parent rosters keep their `[parent,вҖҰchildren]` read-site expansion.

No junction collection (`teams[]` + client `includes()` вҖ” no server `where('teamId')` queries exist). В§ 72.

## 2026-05-22 вҖ” Fix: end-training confirm modal hangs during matcher propagation
**Commit:** `2476cb0` вҖ” merge of `fix/end-training-modal-hang` (`81716d7`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**Bug:** В§ 70 Stage 2 made `updateTraining(status:'closed')` await `propagateTraining` (the multi-source matcher across every matchup of the training вҖ” tens of seconds for a training with many matchups). `MainPage`'s end-training `ConfirmModal` `onConfirm` awaited the whole `updateTraining` before `setEndTrainingConfirm(false)`, so the modal sat open/frozen for the entire propagation run after the user confirmed.

**Fix:** `onConfirm` now dismisses the modal immediately, then runs the close-write + propagation detached (`.catch`-guarded; both already best-effort). The training flips to `closed` the moment `batch.commit()` lands; propagation completes in the background. Only the end-training modal touched вҖ” delete-training (navigates away after) and tournament-close (no batch matcher) left as-is.

**В§ 27:** N/A вҖ” behaviour-only, no UI surface change.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (8.94s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`.

**Found during:** the В§ 70 PPT matcher smoke (training-close fired `propagateTraining`; the modal hung for its duration). That smoke **PASSED** вҖ” see В§ 70.8: 2 PROPAGATED В· 0 BAD, matcher verified, Stage 3 gate cleared.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 2476cb0 && git push && npm run deploy`.

## 2026-05-22 вҖ” Fix: PPT picker shows attendee trainings (В§ 48.2)
**Commit:** `2b88a0a` вҖ” merge of `fix/ppt-picker-attendee-visibility` (`e5032fe`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**Bug:** `usePPTIdentity` filtered the PPT training picker by team alone (`teamIds.includes(tr.teamId)`). A linked player invited as a guest/attendee to **another team's** training was structurally excluded вҖ” the training never appeared in `/player/log`, so they couldn't self-log. (Diagnosed: Koe/RANGER could not see "test training (PROD)", `teamId` 019 Porvoo, despite being in its `attendees[]`.)

**Fix:** the picker filter now admits a training when `teamIds.includes(tr.teamId)` **OR** the player is in `tr.attendees[]`. Dropped the `teamIds`-empty early-return (the OR filter subsumes it). Unlinked path (`if (playerId)`-gated) untouched.

**Side-effect вҖ” unblocks the В§ 70.8 PPT matcher smoke:** every prior self-log smoke used the KIOSK path because the PPT picker never showed the (019 Porvoo) test training to Koe вҶ’ 0 PPT `selfReports` вҶ’ `propagateMatchup` unexercised. Koe can now pick it and PPT-self-log вҶ’ the matcher smoke is finally doable.

**Deferred:** a "guest" tag distinguishing attendee-trainings from own-team cards вҖ” needs `teamIds` threaded to `TrainingCard`; В§ 48.2 follow-up note.

**В§ 27:** N/A вҖ” hook filter change, no UI surface (picker list renders more rows via the unchanged `TrainingCard`).

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (7.58s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`. precommit broken on Windows вҖ” validated directly.

**Smoke:** Koe вҶ’ `/player/log` вҶ’ "test training (PROD)" now appears; own-team (Ranger) trainings still show; unlinked user sees all workspace trainings.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 2b88a0a && git push && npm run deploy`.

## 2026-05-21 вҖ” Fix: dotted-path array destruction in write-back (В§ 70)
**Commit:** `56ee53f` вҖ” merge of `fix/multisource-meta-array-write` (`9c1697f`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**Bug:** `propagateSelfReportToPoint` wrote `_meta` per-slot via dotted field paths (`updateDoc({'homeData.playersMeta.2': вҖҰ})`). Firestore treats the numeric segment as a **map key** вҶ’ it converts the `playersMeta` **array to a map and destroys the other slots' entries**. Found by the В§ 70 Stage 2 smoke: a fully coach-quick-logged point's `awayData.playersMeta` (5 `coach` entries) was reduced to `{"1":kiosk,"3":kiosk}` after two KIOSK self-logs. **Pre-existing** вҖ” KIOSK's `handleKioskSelfLogSave` has done the identical dotted write since Phase 1a; Stage 2's shared fn perpetuated it.

**Fix:** `propagateSelfReportToPoint` reads the point **fresh** and writes **WHOLE** per-slot arrays (`{side}.playersMeta` / `.shotsMeta` / `.eliminationsMeta` / `.players`) via `normaliseSlots` (read-modify-write вҖ” preserves all 5 slots; also repairs map-corruption on fields it touches). The fresh read makes sequential same-point writes correct. Fixes the propagator **and** KIOSK (both use this fn).

**Historical data:** points self-logged before this fix have map-corrupted `_meta` with lost entries вҖ” **unrecoverable**, but it is provenance metadata only (`players`/`assignments`/`eliminations` observation arrays are intact) and nothing reads `_meta` until В§ 70 Stage 3. The fix stops all future loss and repairs points it re-touches.

**Anti-pattern** added to `PROJECT_GUIDELINES.md` В§ 9 (Architektura).

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (5.25s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`. precommit broken on Windows вҖ” validated directly.

**Smoke:** KIOSK self-log a training point вҶ’ after save, that side's `playersMeta` is a 5-element **array** with all coach/scout slots preserved + the self slot tagged (regression check on the touched KIOSK feature).

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 56ee53f && git push && npm run deploy`.

---

## 2026-05-21 вҖ” Klocek 2 В§ 70 Stage 2: matcher + write-back propagator
**Commit:** `184c04c` вҖ” merge of `feat/multisource-stage2-matcher` (`1b4a420` matcher + propagator, `672ec1c` KIOSK adopt, `2c9c3ab` docs)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** Stage 2 of Klocek 2 (В§ 70) вҖ” orphan training `selfReports` are matched to point slots and written back into `homeData/awayData` with `_meta source:'self'`.

- `selfReportMatcher.js` вҖ” pure resolution: `locatePlayerInPoint` (identity вҖ” `assignments.indexOf`), `alignSequence` (temporal 1:1, full-set), `positionConfidence` (`bunkerToPosition` vs `players[slot]`, 12% threshold).
- `dataService.propagateSelfReportToPoint` вҖ” shared write-back (`_meta`, `players[slot]` if empty, shots subcollection, elim meta); `propagateMatchup` / `propagateTraining` вҖ” orchestration, idempotent via `propagatedAt`.
- Triggers: `endMatchupAndMerge` (per matchup) + `updateTraining(status:'closed')` вҶ’ all matchups (Stage 1b). Best-effort вҖ” propagation failure never fails the merge/close.
- KIOSK `handleKioskSelfLogSave` adopts the shared write-back (`source:'kiosk'`).

**Design calls:** per-player subcollection query (no new Firestore index); full-set sequence-align + skip-write (idempotency); `unknown` position вҶ’ write-back; late-log deferred (batch-only вҖ” `updateTraining`-close is the safety net); KIOSK shot `scoutedBy` вҶ’ `linkedUid||playerId`. Conflict = last-writer-wins, `selfReports` immutable.

No rules change. Behaviour: closing a matchup/training now propagates self-logs into the consensus `homeData/awayData`.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (4.65s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`. precommit broken on Windows (bash ENOENT) вҖ” validated directly.

**Smoke:** training вҶ’ coach quick-logs points вҶ’ player PPT self-logs (orphan) вҶ’ close matchup/training вҶ’ `_meta source:'self'` lands in the matched slot, `slotRef`+`propagatedAt` set; re-run = no-op.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 184c04c && git push && npm run deploy`.

**Next:** Stage 1b (free-play coach UI), Stage 3 (granular read + event-scoped aggregation), Stage 4 (manual override UI) вҖ” В§ 70.6.

No entry point, no QuickLogView change вҖ” the "Log free play" UI + squad-less QuickLogView mode are Stage 1b. Training-only вҖ” sparing keeps its natural match.
- **Docs** вҖ” В§ 70 (model + revised stage list 1/1b/2/3/4) + new `docs/architecture/MULTISOURCE_RECONCILIATION.md`.

No rules change (rules don't validate `_meta.source`). Behaviour change is provenance-only вҖ” new QuickLog points carry `source:'coach'`; readers unaffected.

```
Color discipline: N/A вҖ” data layer, no UI/visual surface
Elevation:        N/A
Typography:       N/A
Cards:            N/A
Navigation:       N/A
```

## 2026-05-21 вҖ” KIOSK lobby crash hotfix (Router context)
**Commit:** `1ddafd7` вҖ” merge of `fix/kiosk-lobby-router-context` (`19af7ae`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed

**What changed:** `KioskPostSaveSummary` + `KioskLobbyOverlay` were mounted *after* `</HashRouter>` in `App.jsx`. `KioskLobbyOverlay`'s `useNavigate` (the Brief D "Zobacz swГіj dzieЕ„" deep-link toast) has no Router context outside HashRouter вҶ’ throws `useNavigate() may be used only in the context of a <Router>` вҶ’ crash boundary when the coach taps "PrzekaЕј graczom" to open the KIOSK lobby in a training. The post-save summary survived (no router hook); the lobby crashed. Fix: moved both overlays inside `<HashRouter>` as siblings of `<Routes>` вҖ” still full-screen + self-gated by `KioskContext`, now with Router context.

**Root cause / scope:** latent since Brief D added the `useNavigate` deep-link to the lobby вҖ” surfaced the first time the KIOSK lobby was opened. **Unrelated to В§ 69 / Klocek 2 Stage 1.** Diagnosed read-only first вҖ” the training/point data Jacek created is structurally clean; pure Router-context bug.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (5.94s), `lint-ui` 0 errors. precommit broken on Windows (bash ENOENT) вҖ” validated directly.

**Smoke:** training вҶ’ QuickLog вҶ’ save a point вҶ’ "PrzekaЕј graczom" вҶ’ KIOSK lobby opens without crashing.

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 1ddafd7 && git push && npm run deploy`.

## 2026-05-21 вҖ” Events Model C: events_index (В§ 69)
**Commits:** `41a5ab8` (merge of `feat/events-index-model-c` вҖ” `0396306` dataService writer, `456e05e` useEvents, `10e7f51` rules, `a494634` backfill, `de31bd5` В§ 69 + FIRESTORE_DATA_MODEL.md) + `a2ac142` (backfill dry-run reporting)
**Status:** вң… Deployed вҖ” staged: rules вҶ’ client вҶ’ backfill.

**What changed:** Additive cross-type event index per В§ 69 (Model C вҖ” chosen over Model B full unification). New `/workspaces/{slug}/events_index/{eventId}` вҖ” a 1:1 thin mirror of every tournament/sparing/practice/training, so cross-type readers (PPT picker, player claim flow, aggregation) can list all events without resolving to `/tournaments/` + `/trainings/` or migrating nested trees.

- **Writer** вҖ” `addTournament`/`addTraining` switched `addDoc`вҶ’`doc()+writeBatch`; the index entry is written atomically with the event doc. `updateTournament`/`updateTraining` batch a `setDoc(merge)` index patch. `deleteTournament`/`deleteTraining` drop the index entry in the cascade batch.
- **`useEvents()`** hook + `subscribeEventsIndex` вҖ” additive read surface; the 22 existing consumers + `useTournaments`/`useTrainings` untouched.
- **Rules** вҖ” `events_index` block (read `isMember`, write `isScout`).
- **Docs** вҖ” В§ 69 + new `docs/architecture/FIRESTORE_DATA_MODEL.md` (ground-truth DB map).

**Deploy order corrected from the brief вҖ” rules FIRST.** The index write rides the event-mutation `writeBatch`; with no workspace-level catch-all rule, a client-before-rules deploy would have denied the index write вҶ’ rejected the whole batch вҶ’ broken every event mutation. Sequence: (1) `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` вҖ” clean compile, 0 warnings; (2) `npm run deploy`; (3) backfill.

**Writer verification:** 6 UI-created test events (2 tournament + 2 sparing + 2 training) вҖ” all got atomic index entries, 0 orphans, eventType derivation 100% correct.

**Backfill:** `backfill_events_index.cjs --commit` вҖ” **wrote 14, 0 errors**, count match (14 source = 14 index). Breakdown: 7 tournament / 4 sparing / 0 practice / 3 training. Spot-check (one per type) вҖ” all field mirrors OK, source docs exist, `createdAt` preserved.

**Known issues:** `practice` eventType has zero live data (no `type:'practice'` doc exists in prod) вҖ” dead-discriminator cleanup candidate (NEXT_TASKS). `useEvents` ships with no consumer yet вҖ” PPT-picker rewiring is a follow-up brief.

**Rollback:** client вҖ” `git revert -m 1 41a5ab8 && git push && npm run deploy`; the `events_index` rule + collection are additive (harmless if left).

**Follow-ups:** PPT-picker rewiring to `useEvents`, cross-event aggregation, player claim flow (now unblocked).

## 2026-05-21 вҖ” MembersPage visibility вҖ” elevated-member surfacing (В§ 68)
**Commit:** `955508f` вҖ” merge of `fix/members-visibility-2026-05-20` (2 commits: `34a9991`, `119cc4b`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed
**What changed:** Fixes the 2026-05-20 incident вҖ” the super_admin (Jacek), with `userRoles=[]`, was invisible on `/settings/members` because the active-list filter required `userRoles.length > 0`.

- `MembersPage`: active bucket = non-pending AND (`roles.length>0` OR `isElevated(uid)`), where `isElevated` = `uid===workspace.adminUid` OR (`uid===viewer.uid` AND viewer is super_admin via `useIsSuperAdmin`). Zero extra queries.
- `MemberCard`: new neutral-gray "Admin workspace" status badge for the `adminUid` holder (mirrors the Phase 3.b super_admin badge; non-amber per В§ 27). The `RoleChips` row is skipped when the member has no roles вҖ” elevated members render badge-only, no empty chip row.
- В§ 68 documents the model; NEXT_TASKS gains a 3-item fragility cluster.

**Trimmed scope (Jacek 2026-05-21):** pre-flight discovery corrected two brief assumptions вҖ” the `adminUid` holder is not in `members[]` at all (not a filter issue), and the no-role bucket is 570 members of which 569 are dead post-purge uids. The three-bucket "limbo" design was dropped; a no-role/assignment surface is deferred, blocked on the `members[]` dead-uid prune.

**В§ 27:** PASS вҖ” neutral-gray badge (no amber), reuses the existing badge pattern, one status pill, no other visual surface.

**Validation:** `vite build` вң“ (9.27s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`. precommit broken on Windows (bash ENOENT) вҖ” validated directly.

**Smoke (Jacek, ~1 min):** open `/settings/members` вҶ’ you now appear in the active list with a "Super admin" badge (no role chips вҖ” `userRoles=[]`). The `adminUid` holder `JDDCmHSQвҖҰ` still won't appear вҖ” he is not in `members[]` (the `adminUid`вҶ’non-member anomaly вҖ” NEXT_TASKS fragility cluster).

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 955508f && git push && npm run deploy`.

**Follow-ups (NEXT_TASKS fragility cluster):** `adminUid`вҶ’non-member anomaly В· `members[]` dead-uid prune (569 dead) В· super_admin detection scope.

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” Phase 3.c.2: ownership rules on global /teams/ + /players/
**Commit:** `89d5caf` вҖ” merge of `feat/phase-3-c-2-ownership-rules` (`7f74178` backfill script, `172377e` dataService, `f5adf29` roleUtils, `8e8dda0` rules, `520939c` rollback snapshot)
**Status:** вң… Deployed вҖ” staged Stage 7 (client вҶ’ backfill вҶ’ rules). Stage 7.4 formal smoke partially deferred (below).

**What changed:** Phase 3.c.2 per В§ 65.2 single-owner model + В§ 67. Global `/teams/` + `/players/` create/update were `auth != null` (any authed user) вҖ” now ownership-gated.

- **7.1 client deploy** вҖ” `addTeam`/`addPlayer` write `ownerWorkspaceId` (= workspace slug); `updateTeam`/`updatePlayer` strip it from caller data. Rules not yet live вҶ’ no write breakage.
- **7.2 backfill** вҖ” `phase_3_c_2_ownerworkspaceid.cjs --commit`: **1066 docs** (132 teams + 934 players) set `ownerWorkspaceId = originWorkspace` (all `"ranger1996"`). **0 errors, 0 missing-originWorkspace.** Idempotent + additive.
- **7.3 rules deploy** вҖ” `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules`: **clean compile, 0 warnings**, released. New helper `isWorkspaceAdminOf(slug)`; `/teams/` + `/players/` create = `isSuperAdmin() OR isWorkspaceAdminOf(request ownerWorkspaceId)`, update = same `OR` + an `ownerWorkspaceId`-unchanged immutability clause, delete = `isSuperAdmin()` (unchanged from 3.c.1).
- **Rollback:** `firestore.rules.pre-3c2-backup` (`520939c`) вҶ’ `cp firestore.rules.pre-3c2-backup firestore.rules && firebase deploy --only firestore:rules`.

**Stage 7.4 smoke:** create-team + create-player verified during incident ops 2026-05-20 вҖ” both wrote `ownerWorkspaceId: "ranger1996"`, **passed**. Formal **edit + retire/unretire smoke + the team-delete repro are DEFERRED to the next session** вҖ” rules are live and serving, super_admin path confirmed.

**Notes:** backfill-before-rules is mandatory (rules over un-backfilled docs lock out writes); the backfill is additive/idempotent, safe under either ruleset. Emulator test harness deferred (no JDK вҖ” В§ 67.5). Closure: DESIGN_DECISIONS В§ 65.7.5.

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” UX bug bundle (Bug 1/2/4)
**Commit:** `dc8288e` вҖ” merge of `fix/ux-bugs-bundle-2026-05-20` (3 commits: `13458b2`, `e63ecdf`, `b4db94f`)
**Status:** вң… Deployed
**Diagnosis source:** Live UX session via Claude in Chrome 2026-05-20 17:28вҖ“17:36. Root causes corrected against code at pre-flight вҖ” 3 of the 4 brief hypotheses were off.

**What changed:**
1. **Bug 1 вҖ” WyjdЕә silent fail** (`13458b2`). `LeaveBtn` (Settings вҶ’ Workspace) now disabled for super_admin + the workspace `adminUid` holder, with an explanatory tooltip; `leaveWorkspaceSelf` throws a defensive `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_CANNOT_LEAVE` / `SUPER_ADMIN_CANNOT_LEAVE` guard (handleLeave maps to localized messages); 2 i18n keys Г—PL/EN. Real cause: a self-leave write succeeds but `autoEnterDefaultWorkspace` immediately re-joins the user (ranger1996 is their `defaultWorkspace`) вҖ” a silent no-op. The deeper auto-rejoin loop (hits any non-admin leaving their default workspace) is left as-is вҖ” out of scope for this hotfix.
2. **Bug 2/3 вҖ” MembersPage rows non-tappable** (`e63ecdf`). `MembersPage` derived `isCurrentUserAdmin` from a stale 2-path check (role-array `admin` OR `adminUid`) вҖ” false for Jacek (super_admin via bootstrap, roles `[]`). Switched to `useWorkspace().isAdmin` (4-path, super_admin-aware since Phase 3.a). Row tap now opens `UserDetailPage` (`/settings/members/:uid`, with the Phase 3.b Global role section); inline chips + kebab also unlocked. Navigation was already wired in `MemberCard` вҖ” the brief's "never wired up" hypothesis was wrong; the admin gate was the bug. Bug 3 (no SuperAdmin view) resolves as a consequence.
3. **Bug 4 вҖ” admin-page kebab TypeError** (`b4db94f`). `MoreBtn` (`ui.jsx`) called the consumer `onClick()` with no arguments, so `/admin/teams`, `/admin/players`, `/admin/leagues` handlers doing `(e) => e.stopPropagation?.()` crashed on an undefined `e` (Sentry). Fixed in `MoreBtn` (forwards the event вҖ” systemic) + simplified all 3 admin kebab handlers. The brief's "handler doesn't destructure e" hypothesis was wrong вҖ” `MoreBtn` dropped the event.

**Brief deviations:** Bug 2 + Bug 4 fixes differ from the brief's proposed approach (verified against code per the brief's own "verify before fixing" instruction). Bug 3 needed no separate fix.

**Validation:** `npx vite build` вң“ (8.30s), `lint-ui` 0 errors, 0 `debugger`. precommit broken on Windows (bash ENOENT) вҖ” validated directly.

**Smoke test (Jacek):**
1. Settings вҶ’ Workspace вҶ’ **WyjdЕә disabled** (you're super_admin), tooltip on hover.
2. Settings вҶ’ ДҢlenkowie вҶ’ **tap a member row вҶ’ UserDetailPage** opens with the Global role section.
3. `/admin/teams`, `/admin/players`, `/admin/leagues` вҶ’ **kebab вӢ® opens the ActionSheet**, no Sentry TypeError, no stuck focus border.

**Known issues:** ServiceWorker `register Rejected` (separate Sentry ticket, lower priority вҖ” not in scope).

**Rollback:** `git revert -m 1 dc8288e && git push && npm run deploy`.

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” Phase 3.c.1: Rules helpers refactor + super_admin awareness (В§ 67)
**Commit:** `0aac3c1` (rules + В§ 67) + follow-up (drop unused isViewer + ship docs)
**Status:** вң… Rules deployed via `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` вҖ” compiled clean, released. No client deploy.

**What changed:** Phase 3.c sub-task 1 per В§ 67.7. Backwards-compatible `firestore.rules` refactor вҖ” zero behaviour change for current production users.

- `isBootstrapAdmin()` вҖ” centralizes the ADMIN_EMAILS allowlist (the one place the hardcoded email lives).
- `isSuperAdmin()` вҖ” `isBootstrapAdmin()` OR `users/{uid}.globalRole == 'super_admin'` (Phase 3.a field; `exists()`-guarded `get()`).
- `isAdmin(slug)` вҖ” now 4-path: `isSuperAdmin()` OR role `'admin'` OR `adminUid`.
- 5 hardcoded `token.email == jacek` sites centralized via `isSuperAdmin()` (isAdmin path + `/users/` disable + `/leagues/` write + `/players/` delete + `/teams/` delete).
- Removed dead `/notes/{nid}` block вҖ” no dataService writer; real coach notes live at `tournaments/{tid}/scouted/{sid}/notes/` (tournament catch-all = `isScout`).
- В§ 67 Firestore Rules Architecture + В§ 65.7.4 closure docs.

**Backwards compatible вҖ” zero behaviour change:**
- Jacek: admin via bootstrap (email) AND `globalRole='super_admin'` вҖ” both fire, identical access.
- Workspace coach/scout/admin: paths 1-2 (role array, adminUid) untouched.
- A future non-Jacek super_admin (globalRole set) now gains rule-level super_admin access вҖ” intended new capability; no such user exists yet.
- `/notes/` block removal: zero impact вҖ” no docs, no writers at that path.

**isViewer dropped (post-deploy correction):** the first deploy added `isViewer()` (brief specified it "for 3.c.2") вҖ” but an unused rules function emits a Firestore-compiler warning plus a spurious knock-on "Invalid variable name" warning. Confirmed unused-function artifact (`isPlayer` / `isSelfLogShotOwned` use the identical `request.auth.uid` pattern with zero warnings). Removed `isViewer`; it lands in 3.c.2 with its first match-block consumer. Second deploy: clean compile, zero warnings.

**Test harness deferred (Jacek decision):** build machine has no JDK; the Firestore emulator requires one. `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` harness is a follow-up gated on JDK availability вҖ” В§ 67.5. 3.c.1 rules validated by deploy-time compilation (clean) + smoke test вҖ” the Phase 2.x pattern.

**No client code:** `src/` untouched вҖ” vite build not re-run (`firestore.rules` is not part of the bundle).

**Smoke test (Jacek, ~3 min):**
1. Refresh app вҖ” admin routes work (`/admin/leagues`, `/admin/players`, `/admin/teams`).
2. UserDetailPage "Global role" section toggles (Phase 3.b regression check).
3. As a coach (non-super-admin) вҖ” workspace data reads/writes unchanged.
4. Sentry: zero new permission-denied errors in first 24h.
5. В§ 67 + В§ 65.7.4 visible in DESIGN_DECISIONS.md.

**Rollback:** revert the two Phase 3.c.1 commits + `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules`. Emergency last resort: `firestore.rules.backup` (pre-В§38 ruleset).

**Unlocks:** 3.c.2 (global `/players/`+`/teams/` create/update hardening [HIGH RISK]), 3.c.3 (PII scoping).

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” Phase 3.b: super_admin globalRole editing (scope reconciled)
**Commit:** `bddeb10`
**Status:** вң… Deployed (autonomous, no rules changes)

**What changed:** Phase 3.b scope reconciled at pre-flight (В§ 66.8 lesson). The brief proposed a new `/admin/users` super-admin console; CC discovery found it would ~80% duplicate existing workspace member-management UI вҖ” `MembersPage` (`/settings/members`), `UserDetailPage` (`/settings/members/:uid`, В§ 50.4), `MemberCard` inline role editing, `RoleChips`, `RoleTransferModal`, dataService `updateUserRoles`/`removeMember`/`transferAdmin`/`softDisableUser`. Those helpers hardcode the current workspace via `bp()`, and production runs a single workspace вҖ” a cross-workspace console has no consumer yet. Jacek chose the minimal path: extend existing pages with the one genuinely-new capability вҖ” `globalRole` editing.

1. `ds.setUserGlobalRole(uid, role)` вҖ” writes `/users/{uid}.globalRole`, validates role вҲҲ {'super_admin', null}.
2. `UserDetailPage` вҖ” new "Global role" section between Roles and the danger zone, gated by `useIsSuperAdmin()` (super_admin only, В§ 65.3 Q1). Radio (Standard user / Super admin) + `ConfirmModal` on every change. **First UI consumer of the Phase 3.a `useIsSuperAdmin` hook** вҖ” validates 3.a end-to-end.
3. `MemberCard` вҖ” neutral-gray "SUPER ADMIN" status badge (non-interactive вҶ’ not amber per В§ 27).
4. `useUserProfiles` extended to expose `globalRole`.
5. 11 i18n keys (PL + EN).
6. В§ 65.7.3 doc patch.

**Preserved per В§ 66.6 anti-patterns:** NO new `/admin/users` route, NO AdminUsersPage / UserFormModal / SuperAdminGuard / useAllUsers, NO duplicate dataService helpers, NO schema beyond Phase 3.a's `globalRole`. `MembersPage`/`UserDetailPage`/`MemberCard`/`RoleChips`/`workspace.userRoles[uid]` unchanged in behaviour. `PendingApprovalPage` reviewed вҖ” already В§ 27-compliant, no polish needed (brief Step H skipped).

**Deferred (no consumer in single-tenant production):**
- Dedicated cross-workspace `/admin/users` console вҖ” re-brief when workspace #2 onboards
- Self-revoke guard on the Global role section вҖ” irrelevant while the only super_admin (Jacek) is ADMIN_EMAILS-protected

**Migration coupling:** Phase 3.a `globalRole` migration still pending (deferred вҖ” no service account). 3.b works regardless: `useIsSuperAdmin` resolves Jacek via the ADMIN_EMAILS fallback, so the Global role section is visible to him now; editing a user's globalRole writes the field on demand. Running the 3.a migration just back-fills `globalRole=null` on docs missing it вҖ” cosmetic.

**Smoke test (Jacek, ~3 min):**
1. `/settings/members` вҶ’ tap any member вҶ’ UserDetailPage. A "Global role" section appears between Roles and the danger zone.
2. Tap "Super admin" on a test user вҶ’ ConfirmModal вҶ’ confirm вҶ’ Firestore Console `/users/{uid}.globalRole === 'super_admin'`.
3. Back to `/settings/members` вҶ’ that row shows a gray "SUPER ADMIN" badge.
4. Re-open the test user вҶ’ "Standard user" вҶ’ confirm вҶ’ `globalRole` back to null, badge gone.
5. В§ 65.7.3 visible in DESIGN_DECISIONS.md. Sentry: zero new errors.

**Rollback:** `git revert bddeb10 && git push && npm run deploy`. globalRole values set via the UI are reversible via the same UI.

**Tooling note:** precommit broken on Windows (bash ENOENT). Validated directly: vite build вң“ (5.86s), lint-ui 0 errors, 0 debugger.

**Unlocks:** Phase 3.c вҖ” Firestore rules refactor [HIGH RISK]; globalRole is now editable, rules can gate against it.

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” Phase 3.a: globalRole field + isAdmin 4th path + useIsSuperAdmin
**Commit:** `8f77d62`
**Status:** вң… Code deployed (autonomous, no rules changes). вң… Migration run 2026-05-20.

**What changed:** Surgical Phase 3.a per В§ 66.5 reconciled scope. Original brief halted in `80bcb16` (greenfield-assumption conflict); В§ 66 reconciliation (`72d601c`) cleared the path.

1. `users.globalRole: 'super_admin' | null` field вҖ” additive. Absent on pre-migration docs reads falsy in the 4th path вҶ’ identical to pre-3.a behaviour.
2. `isAdmin(workspace, user, userProfile?)` вҖ” 4th path `userProfile?.globalRole === 'super_admin'`. 3rd arg optional, defaults null вҶ’ all existing 2-arg call sites unchanged.
3. `isSuperAdmin(user, userProfile)` helper exported вҖ” globalRole field OR ADMIN_EMAILS bootstrap fallback. Cross-workspace gate, distinct from workspace-scoped isAdmin.
4. `useIsSuperAdmin()` hook вҖ” new file `src/hooks/useIsSuperAdmin.js`. No 3.a consumers; ships for Phase 3.b-f.
5. `src/hooks/useWorkspace.jsx` вҖ” both `isAdmin` util call sites (adminFlag useMemo + migrateWorkspaceRoles trigger) pass `userProfile` through.
6. `scripts/migration/phase_3_a_globalrole.cjs` вҖ” idempotent, gated by `PHASE_3_A_EXECUTE_CONFIRMED`, repo-standard `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` init.
7. В§ 65.7.2 doc patch documents 3.a closure.

**Preserved per В§ 66.6 anti-patterns:** 5 backend roles (admin/coach/scout/viewer/player), `workspace.userRoles[uid]`, `workspace.adminUid`, `ADMIN_EMAILS`, `PendingApprovalPage`, `isPendingApproval`, `canAccessRoute`, `useViewAs` / `AdminGuard` / `useFeatureFlag` (4th path cascades automatically via useWorkspace), `getOrCreateUserProfile` defaults, Firestore rules вҖ” all unchanged.

**Deviations from brief (per pre-flight HALT):**
- Migration script uses repo pattern (`GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` + `EXECUTE_CONFIRMED` gate + JSON report) not the brief's `dotenv`/`FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY` template.
- `useIsSuperAdmin` lives in its own file (role-hook convention вҖ” mirrors useViewAs.js) not `useFirestore.js`.
- Both `isAdmin` util call sites updated for consistency (brief design D mentioned only `adminFlag`).

**вң… Migration RUN 2026-05-20.** `phase_3_a_globalrole.cjs` executed against production вҖ” 21 `/users/` docs: `globalRole='super_admin'` for Jacek, `null` for the other 20. Verified: super_admin 1, null 20, absent 0 вҖ” every doc has the field explicitly. Reports: `scripts/migration/reports/phase_3_a_globalrole_*.json`. The first run set Jacek but skipped the 20 absent docs (the idempotency check collapsed absent вүЎ null); the check was corrected to write explicit `null` to absent docs вҖ” important because Firestore rules referencing a missing `resource.data.globalRole` error and deny, so Phase 3.c needs the field on every doc вҖ” and the second run completed all 21. Script is idempotent on further re-runs.

**Smoke verify (Jacek):**
1. Refresh app вҖ” admin routes work (`/admin/leagues`, `/admin/players`, `/admin/teams`)
2. Existing coach/scout users вҖ” app unchanged
3. Sentry: zero new errors in first 24h
4. В§ 65.7.2 visible at bottom of В§ 65 in DESIGN_DECISIONS.md
5. (post-migration) Firestore Console: `/users/{jacek}` `globalRole='super_admin'`, other docs `globalRole=null`

**Rollback:** `git revert 8f77d62 && git push && npm run deploy`. If the migration has run, the additive `globalRole` field is harmless вҖ” reverted code ignores it.

**Unlocks:** Phase 3.b (super_admin user mgmt UI вҖ” first `useIsSuperAdmin` consumer), 3.c-f per В§ 66.5.

**Tooling note:** `scripts/precommit.js` broken on Windows (bash ENOENT). Validated directly: `npx vite build` вң“ (5.34s), `node scripts/lint-ui.js` 0 errors, `grep debugger` 0 hits.

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” В§ 66 В§ 65вҶ”В§ 38 reconciliation (doc-only)
**Commit:** `docs: В§ 66 вҖ” В§ 65 вҶ” В§ 38 reconciliation, unblocks Phase 3.a` (2026-05-20 вҖ” find via `git log --grep "В§ 66"`)

**What changed:** Added В§ 66 to `docs/DESIGN_DECISIONS.md`. Documents how В§ 65 (Permissions Architecture, semantic role names + ownership model + Q1-Q4) maps to В§ 38 v2.1 (operational role data model, mature 6 months). Closes the conflict surfaced by CC pre-flight escalation 2026-05-20 (commit `80bcb16` halt). NEXT_TASKS Phase 3.a flipped рҹҡ§ вҶ’ рҹҺҜ вҖ” code brief rewrites against В§ 66 next Opus session.

**What В§ 66 codifies:**
- Mapping table: В§ 65 super_admin/workspace_admin/coach/scout/pending_user вҶ” В§ 38 backend (workspace.userRoles[uid] + workspace.adminUid + ADMIN_EMAILS + users.globalRole-to-be-added + isPendingApproval)
- Roles in В§ 38 not enumerated by В§ 65: viewer (retired per В§ 49), player (PPT self-logging)
- Backend SOT: В§ 38 data model + helper functions (isAdmin 3-path, isPendingApproval, canAccessRoute)
- Phase 3 sub-task plan now written against В§ 38 backend using В§ 65 semantics
- Anti-patterns specific to this conflict (6 NEVER rules)
- В§ 66.8 вҖ” root cause + lesson for future Opus permission/role design

**Operational truth post-commit:**
- В§ 38 + В§ 49 = live, mature, operational truth (unchanged)
- В§ 65 = forward-looking permission semantics + ownership + Q1-Q4 (banner from CC halt remains)
- В§ 66 = THE bridge вҖ” authoritative mapping reference for Phase 3.b-f briefs
- Phase 3.g (Vision OCR disable, commit `2997cca`) stays shipped вҖ” independent

**Smoke verify (Jacek, 1 min):**

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” В§ 65 Permissions Architecture + AI Vision OCR import disabled
**Commit:** `2997cca`
**Status:** вң… Deployed (autonomous, no Firestore rules changes)

**What changed:** Pre-Phase-3 prep вҖ” two surgical changes in one commit.

**1. DESIGN_DECISIONS В§ 65 Permissions Architecture (locked 2026-05-20):**
- 5 roles: super_admin / workspace_admin / coach / scout / pending_user
- Teams: single owner via `ownerWorkspaceId` (Phase 3 addition); `originWorkspace` is **audit only**, NOT authorization signal
- Players: tri-mode editing вҖ” PBLeagues canonical (super_admin only) / manually created (workspace_admin in own workspace) / annotations subcollection (Phase 3.1+ deferred)
- Full resource Г— operation matrix (rows = operations, cols = roles) вҖ” authoritative source for Phase 3 Firestore rules refactor + UI gating logic
- Q1-Q4 resolutions (locked from 2026-05-20 chat): Q1 super_admin-only user mgmt; Q2 ownership MVP + annotations deferred; Q3 disable AI for import + Phase 3 data isolation via rules; Q4 open reads on canonical / strict PII + workspace data
- 9 anti-patterns codified (no `originWorkspace` authz / no self-elevation / no client-side Anthropic key / no cross-workspace writes by workspace_admin / etc.)
- Phase 3 sub-task plan: 3.a role schema + migration вҶ’ 3.b pending user UI вҶ’ 3.c rules refactor (HIGH risk) вҶ’ 3.d workspace admin UI вҶ’ 3.e player editing model вҶ’ 3.f team ownership UI вҶ’ 3.g Vision OCR disable (this commit) вҶ’ 3.1+ annotations layer (deferred)
- Brief asked for В§ 64 but that number was already taken by Canvas Architecture (approved 2026-05-19) вҖ” landed as **В§ 65** per brief's escalation #3 with all internal anchors renumbered (В§ 64.x вҶ’ В§ 65.x). Section-numbering note included at top of new section.

**2. AI Vision OCR client-side calls DISABLED вҖ” `STATIC_FLAGS.ENABLE_VISION_API` flipped to `false`:**
- **Layout Wizard:** Vision Scan step (step 3) hidden. Wizard now **2-step** (Basic Info вҶ’ Calibrate вҶ’ Finish navigates to bunker editor for manual entry). ProgressBar `total={2}` instead of `total={3}`. Subtitle "STEP N OF 2" instead of "STEP N OF 3".
- **LayoutDetailPage OCRBunkerDetect modal:** render block gated. `setOcrOpen(true)` had no callers anywhere вҖ” dead code from prior work. Gate adds defense in depth against future rewire.
- **ScoutTabContent "Import schedule (zdjДҷcie)" Btn:** hidden. Underlying `ScheduleImport` render also gated. **`ScheduleCSVImport` ("Import harmonogramu (CSV)") UNAFFECTED** вҖ” manual schedule import path continues working.
- All 3 Anthropic API call sites (`VisionScan.jsx`, `OCRBunkerDetect.jsx`, `ScheduleImport.jsx`) get **early-return guards** at function entry. localStorage API key reads stay in place (preserved for future server-side re-impl), just no-op'd by flag. Comment block at each site references В§ 65 + future Cloud Function migration path.

**Scope expansion vs brief:** brief enumerated 2 affordances (Layout Wizard + вӢ® menu); 3rd live Anthropic call site (ScheduleImport) added per Jacek pre-confirmation go-ahead вҖ” В§ 65.5 anti-pattern "Bundle Anthropic API key in client bundle" applies uniformly. Brief's "ENABLE_VISION_API flag" was also acknowledged in pre-confirmation as existing-but-unused вҖ” repurposed cleanly (flip to false + plumb through) instead of adding redundant `visionOcrImport`.

**Defense in depth (3 layers per Vision OCR site):**
- L1 UI: button/affordance hidden conditional on flag
- L2 render: component render block gated by flag
- L3 fn entry: early-return inside async handler if flag off

**Existing Vision-scanned layouts:** continue rendering correctly. Vision OCR only ran at LAYOUT CREATION time to seed initial bunker positions вҖ” once positions saved to Firestore they're stable data with zero ongoing dependency on Vision API. Manual editing path (`/layout/{id}/bunkers`) unaffected.

**NO Firestore rules changes. NO schema changes. NO new components.** Pure doc + feature flag + UI gating + 3 function early-returns + import additions. 8 files changed, +265/-12. Bundle size unchanged (vision code still compiled, just behind flag вҖ” gives instant re-enable via flag flip after Cloud Function migration).

**Smoke test required (Jacek) вҖ” 5 quick checks:**
1. Open Layout Wizard (`/layout/new`) вҶ’ confirm only 2 steps (Basic Info вҶ’ Calibrate вҶ’ Finish). Header reads "STEP N OF 2". ProgressBar has 2 segments.
2. Step 2 "Finish вҶ’" creates layout and navigates to `/layout/{id}/bunkers` (manual bunker entry) вҖ” no Vision Scan step appears.
3. Open Scout tab вҶ’ tournament view вҶ’ confirm "Import schedule (zdjДҷcie)" Btn ABSENT, "Import harmonogramu (CSV)" Btn still present.
4. Open an existing Vision-scanned layout вҖ” bunkers render correctly (no API call, just reading stored positions).
5. Sentry: zero errors in first 24h.
6. DESIGN_DECISIONS.md: scroll to bottom, verify В§ 65 visible with matrix table rendering correctly, no markdown breakage.

**Rollback path:** `git revert 2997cca && git push && npm run deploy`. Feature flag also serves as runtime kill switch (flip back to `true` + redeploy = re-enable everything, code preserved).

**Re-enable path (Phase 3+):** requires server-side Cloud Function migration. Per В§ 65.5 anti-pattern client-side Anthropic key MUST NOT be re-bundled. Re-enabling flag without server-side migration re-opens the same attack surface.

**Unlocks:**
- Phase 2.4 TeamMemberships design (ownership semantics defined in В§ 65.2)
- Phase 3 implementation track (clear sub-task ordering in В§ 65.6)
- Tenant onboarding planning (Paintball FIT example workflow clear once Phase 3.a-d ship)

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” Phase 2.3.c: Super admin UI for Teams CRUD (sister team + duplicate resolution)
**Commit:** `6638c54`
**Status:** вң… Deployed (Jacek smoke test required)

**What changed:** Super admin UI at `/admin/teams` for managing global `/teams/` collection (132 docs). MVP scope: searchable + filterable + paginated list (50/page), create + edit modal with **sister team designation** (both parent + children directions, card-style picker via TeamPickerModal, cycle prevention), **externalId duplicate resolution flow** (side-by-side comparison + weighted recommendation heuristic + dynamic safety note), **soft delete via retiredAt** timestamp (NOT hard delete вҖ” preserves audit trail + reference resolution). Closes Phase 2 Step 3 (Teams) consumption + admin path; only 2.3.d cleanup deferred. Reuses Phase 2.3.b `useTeams` + dual-write `dataService` + Phase 2.1c admin gate pattern. **No Firestore rules update** вҖ” Phase 2.3.b already covered admin writes + admin email delete gate. Doc patch В§ 63.15.2.X.1 locks UX decisions from chat mockup review.

**Defense in depth admin gate (3 layers):**
- L1 route: `<AdminGuard>` wraps `/admin/teams` route (App.jsx)
- L2 component: `if (!effectiveIsAdmin) return null` in AdminTeamsPage
- L3 Firestore rules: `/teams/{teamId}` delete restricted to `jacek@epicsports.pl` (from Phase 2.3.b)

**Schema additions (additive вҖ” existing 132 docs treat absent fields as active):**
- `retiredAt: Timestamp | null`
- `retiredBy: uid | null`
- `retirementReason: string | null`
- `canonicalReplacementId: docId | null` (pointer to canonical when retired via duplicate resolution)

**New `useActiveTeams()` hook (asymmetric design):**
- `teams`: array filtered to `retiredAt == null` (for iteration in pickers/lists)
- `teamsById`: map of ALL teams incl. retired (for spot lookups вҖ” MatchPage opponent display, PlayerStatsPage player.teamId, etc., avoiding "Unknown" rendering when team retired after reference written)
- 21 React consumers refactored from `useTeams` вҶ’ `useActiveTeams` (mechanical token-level replace)
- AdminTeamsPage stays on raw `useTeams` (admin needs to see retired)

**Duplicate resolution heuristic** (mockup-locked per В§ 63.15.2.X.1):
- Weighted score: `children Г— 100 + tournamentRefs Г— 5 + playerRefs Г— 1 + recency (0вҖ“50)`
- Tournament refs DEFERRED (вҖ” placeholder) вҖ” for the 1 known case (RANGER vs Ranger Warsaw) children alone (3 children Г— 100 = 300 vs 0 = 0) makes recommendation unambiguous
- Top scorer gets `RECOMMENDED` green badge
- Admin can override via radio

**Children orphan safety on retire** (ChildrenOrphanWarning component):
- Enhanced ConfirmModal when retiring team with active children
- 3 radio options: re-point to selected new parent (recommended for dup cleanup) / cascade retire children / orphan (do nothing вҖ” references still resolve)
- Mirrors Phase 2.2.c aliasIds safety pattern, adapted for parent-child

**Reference re-pointing DEFERRED per В§ 63.15.2.X.1 / Phase 2.3.d:**
- Resolution view checkbox "Re-point tournament/player references" shown DISABLED with explanation
- Retired team docs remain queryable вҖ” references continue resolving via teamsById map (asymmetric design preserves retired teams in spot lookups)

**Bundle:** AdminTeamsPage chunk lazy-loaded вҖ” zero cost for non-admin users.

**Smoke test required (Jacek) вҖ” 17 steps for the new admin flow:**
1. Open `/admin/teams` from More tab вҶ’ Admin вҶ’ Teams вҖ” verify access (admin only)
2. List loads with 132 active teams (no retired by default вҖ” filter pill `Active` set)
3. Banner at top: `вҡ  1 externalId duplicate detected. [Review duplicates вҶ’]`
4. Tap "Review вҶ’" вҶ’ filter switches to `вҡ  Duplicates` вҶ’ RANGER + Ranger Warsaw both shown with `вҡ ` prefix
5. Per-row MoreBtn on RANGER вҶ’ ActionSheet вҶ’ "Resolve duplicate вҶ’" вҶ’ resolution view opens
6. RANGER card has `RECOMMENDED` green badge (3 children Г— 100 = 300 points vs Ranger Warsaw's 0)
7. Score breakdown line shows: `kids 300 + plyrs N + recency M`
8. Pick RANGER as canonical (radio) вҶ’ "Retire other" checked by default вҶ’ "Re-point children" greyed N/A (RANGER has children, not Ranger Warsaw) вҶ’ "Re-point references" DISABLED with explanation
9. Safety note: green `вң“ ... safe.` (canonical RANGER preserves children)
10. Confirm resolution вҶ’ Ranger Warsaw retired, list refreshes, banner gone
11. Switch filter pill to `рҹ—„ Retired` вҶ’ Ranger Warsaw visible there with retiredAt + canonicalReplacementId in audit section
12. MoreBtn on Ranger Warsaw вҶ’ "Restore" вҶ’ retiredAt cleared вҶ’ back in active list (banner reappears since dup detected again)
13. Edit a child team (RING) вҶ’ Sister team section shows RANGER as Parent card with Change/Remove actions вҶ’ tap "Change в–ҫ" вҶ’ TeamPickerModal opens with searchable list excluding RING + RING's descendants + retired
14. Edit RANGER (parent) вҶ’ Sister team section shows Children list (RAGE/RING/RUSH) with individual Remove buttons
15. Try to set RING as parent of RANGER via picker (would create cycle) вҶ’ save rejected with error toast "Cycle detected вҖ” proposed parent is descendant of this team"
16. Manual retire RANGER via TeamFormModal footer "Retire team" вҶ’ ChildrenOrphanWarning modal opens with 3-option radio (rePoint / cascade / orphan) + new-parent picker
17. Verify NewTournamentModal team picker no longer shows retired teams (useActiveTeams filter working); MatchPage opponent display still resolves if team retired after match created (teamsById preserves all)

**Sentry watch:** zero `setParentTeam`/`retireTeam`/`unretireTeam` errors in first 24h.

**Rollback path:** `git revert 6638c54 && git push && npm run deploy`. Schema additions (retiredAt etc.) remain on docs that were edited via the new UI вҖ” additive, no harm. If admin retired a team mid-rollback, the doc retains retiredAt but consumers post-revert use raw useTeams again so retired teams reappear in lists (intended cushion).

**Unlocks:** Phase 2 Step 3 effectively CLOSED. Phase 2.4 (TeamMemberships junction) writeable вҖ” can reference global team IDs with active filter via useActiveTeams. Phase 2.3.d cleanup is the only Step 3 remainder.

## 2026-05-20 вҖ” Phase 2.3.b: useTeams global + dual-write + /teams/ rules
**Commit:** `97af95a`
**Status:** вң… DEPLOYED 2026-05-20 by Jacek (sequenced: firebase deploy --only firestore:rules вҶ’ npm run deploy вҶ’ hard refresh)

**What changed:** React team consumers migrated from workspace path to global `/teams/` (populated by Phase 2.3.a, commit `3d8ea9c`). Hook refactor in `src/hooks/useFirestore.js:65` вҖ” `useTeams()` now reads global `/teams/` via onSnapshot, returns `{ teams, teamsById, loading, error }` (additive вҖ” existing `{ teams, loading }` destructures keep working in all 20 consumers; `teamsById` map provides O(1) lookup for parentTeamId resolution). Sentry on fetch error. `dataService.js` `addTeam` + `updateTeam` dual-write to both global + legacy workspace paths (mirror Phase 2.2.b pattern). `deleteTeam` workspace-only вҖ” global delete deferred to Phase 2.3.c admin UI (parentTeamId children + externalId duplicate safety). `firestore.rules` adds `/teams/{teamId}` block (read auth, create+update auth, delete admin email вҖ” mirror Phase 2.2.b `/players/` pattern); `/leagues/` from Phase 2.1c + `/players/` from Phase 2.2.b preserved unchanged. **NO `npm run deploy` from CC** вҖ” Jacek must run sequenced rules-first deploy.

**Read scope collapsed massively:** all 20 React consumers (TeamsPage, TeamDetailPage, MatchPage, ScoutedTeamPage, MainPage, MembersPage, PlayersPage, UserDetailPage, TrainingSetupPage, PlayerPerformanceTrackerPage, ProfilePage, PlayerStatsPage, NewTournamentModal, TournamentPicker, AttendeesEditor, ViewAsPlayerPicker, LinkProfileModal, CoachTabContent, ScoutTabContent, TrainingMoreTab, usePPTIdentity) already use the centralized `useTeams` hook вҶ’ **zero consumer file changes for the read path**. Identical setup to Phase 2.2.b which had usePlayers also centralized.

**Write dual-write scope:** 9 call sites unchanged (TeamsPage:62/66, TeamDetailPage 93/100/112/153, CSVImport 283/288, ScheduleCSVImport:384, ScheduleImport:242) вҖ” all go through `dataService.addTeam/updateTeam/deleteTeam` which now dual-write transparently. originWorkspace tagged on new global writes (matches Phase 2.3.a bootstrap schema).

**breakoutVariants subcollection untouched** per В§ 63.15.2 "workspace-specific scouting context" decision in Phase 2.3.a pre-flight вҖ” 4 dataService functions still read/write via `bp()` workspace path; legacy `/workspaces/{slug}/teams/` dual-write keeps parent docs alive so subcollection reads work unchanged.

**рҹҡЁ ACTION REQUIRED вҖ” JACEK SEQUENCED DEPLOY:**
```bash
git pull origin main

# Verify all 3 global rule blocks present (5 total matches with workspace nested)
# In PowerShell: Select-String -Path firestore.rules -Pattern "match /leagues/|match /players/|match /teams/" -Context 1,4

# 1. RULES FIRST
firebase deploy --only firestore:rules

# 2. Verify Firebase Console вҶ’ Firestore вҶ’ Rules tab shows fresh "Last published" timestamp

# 3. CODE
npm run deploy

# 4. Hard refresh production
#    (Ctrl+F5 on Windows; or DevTools open + right-click reload вҶ’ "Empty Cache and Hard Reload")
```

**Why sequenced matters:** if code deploys before rules вҶ’ all `/teams/` reads return permission-denied вҶ’ `useTeams` hook returns empty `[]` вҶ’ TeamsPage shows no teams, NewTournamentModal team picker empty, MatchPage opponent name blank, match cards broken in Scout tab, etc. Estimated 1-2 minute blast radius if order reversed (entire user base sees broken team rendering until rules deploy completes).

**Smoke test (post-deploy):**
1. Open https://epicsports.github.io/pbscoutpro after hard refresh
2. Open TeamsPage вҶ’ verify all **132 teams** render with parent-child grouping
3. Spot-check parent-children: **RANGER** (docId `7JNZJNlaSmRk4BVTfaJK`) should expand to **RAGE/RING/RUSH** child rows
4. Open TeamDetailPage for a child team (e.g. RING `HusyOerlWDC4Cn5FHB8G`) вҶ’ verify parent reference (RANGER name shows somewhere)
5. Open NewTournamentModal вҶ’ team picker shows teams filtered by league/division
6. Open existing match вҶ’ homeTeam/awayTeam names render correctly
7. Edit a test team via TeamDetailPage (e.g. add a comment) вҶ’ save вҶ’ verify update propagates live to TeamsPage (onSnapshot + dual-write)
8. Verify Coach tab still loads scoutedTeams + opponent names render
9. DevTools Network: `/teams` Firestore reads return 200 with snapshot data; zero permission-denied errors
10. Sentry: zero `useTeams fetch failed` errors in first 24h
11. **Specific externalId dup verification:** TeamsPage list contains BOTH `RANGER` (parent of children) AND `Ranger Warsaw` (orphan parent) вҖ” both visible as expected per В§ 63.15.2.X #7 (admin curates one or the other via future Phase 2.3.c)

**Rollback path (if smoke fails):**
- Code revert: `git revert 97af95a && git push && npm run deploy`
- Rules revert: edit firestore.rules to remove `/teams/` block + `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules`
- `/teams/` data unchanged either way вҖ” Phase 2.3.a 132 global docs intact, workspace `/workspaces/ranger1996/teams/` source still live for legacy reads (which kick back in if hook reverts)

**Known issues / deferred:**
- Dual-write means team mutations now 2x Firestore writes (acceptable transition cost вҖ” Phase 2.3.d collapses to global-only after consumption stabilizes)
- Empty/loading state per consumer вҖ” first render shows empty `teams` briefly while hook fetches (same Option-A pattern as Phase 2.2.b post-deploy; no consumer regression observed there)
- Phase 2.3.c admin UI required for global team curation (currently teams creatable via workspace UI with transparent dual-write but no admin governance) вҖ” must include sister team designation UI per В§ 63.15.2.X #3 + RANGER/Ranger Warsaw merge resolution
- Phase 2.3.d cleanup deferred вҖ” workspace `/workspaces/{slug}/teams/` subcollection remains in sync via dual-write until 2.3.c stable
- `subscribeTeams` in dataService.js:259 now orphan (no callers post-hook-refactor) вҖ” kept as dead code mirror of `subscribePlayers` orphan; deferred to Phase 2.3.d cleanup

**Post-deploy follow-up commit (CC after Jacek smoke pass):**
- HANDOVER.md update (Recently shipped row + Track B status + Last-updated)
- MULTI_TENANT_MIGRATION_PLAN.md sub-task b marked вң…
- NEXT_TASKS.md flip вҸі вҶ’ вң…, promote 2.3.c to active рҹҺҜ
- DEPLOY_LOG status flip вҸі вҶ’ вң…
